asset_family: town forge equipment direction target and source families
source_family_role: full-screen direction target for town forge/equipment upgrade; not a fused runtime asset
asset_type: game fake shot / visual target, not final runtime UI
prompt: Create a polished 16:9 fantasy browser RPG screen for Ember Road's town forge upgrade moment. The player returned from the Old Mine cache, spends ember shards, forges or equips the Mine Lantern, and opens the next mine depth. Main scene: Old Gate town square with a visible forge/workbench near the gate, warm firelight, anvil or smithing table, Mine Lantern object glowing on the bench, ember shard resource glow, road and mine route markers visible. Hero stands near the forge. A blacksmith or Gate Warden is present as the upgrade focus. The lantern result and Depth 2 route promise are visible before reading UI text. Painterly 2D fantasy browser RPG, ornate but restrained frame, readable silhouettes, weathered stone, leather, brass, iron, parchment, ember gold accents. 1280x720 landscape. Main painted location takes the left two-thirds. Right rail is an equipment/result summary, not the main fantasy. Route strip shows Old Gate, North Road, Old Mine, and a Depth 2 open or lit marker. Bottom log belt is present but secondary confirmation. Leave blank content areas where runtime text, costs, state values, and labels will be overlaid. No readable text, no fake letters, no numbers.
negative_prompt: debug rectangles, flat programmer art, modern mobile VIP/event clutter, gacha formation UI, city-builder UI, idle or wait timers, unreadable pseudo-text, watermarks, logos, stock-photo look, panel-only shop UX, one-color purple/blue palette, copied War of Dragons UI, copied Dragon Eternity UI, copied DragonFable art, copied AdventureQuest art, copied AQW art, copied Shakes and Fidget art, copied Wartune UI, copied Hero Wars UI, readable labels, baked counters, fake glyphs, numbers inside cost/result slots, Y-up assumptions inverted
runtime_text_policy: all labels, counters, costs, and state values must be blank for runtime composition
coordinate_policy: Y-up in game/UI logic; renderer, input, screenshot, and DevAPI convert at boundaries only
acceptance: forge/workbench and Mine Lantern visible in the main scene
hero and upgrade NPC/focus readable at gameplay size
ember shard cost, lantern result, and Depth 2 unlock have visual anchors
right rail summarizes the event but does not carry the whole UX
bottom log confirms the event but is not the only reward feedback
no baked labels, fake text, watermark, or copied reference art
acceptance_checklist: Forge/workbench and Mine Lantern are visible in the main scene.
Hero and upgrade NPC/focus are readable at gameplay size.
Ember shard cost, lantern result, and Depth 2 unlock have visual anchors.
Right rail summarizes the event but does not carry the whole UX.
Bottom log confirms the event but is not the only reward feedback.
No baked labels, fake text, watermark, or copied reference art.
Y-up is preserved in layout notes and runtime translation.
